_comment: Survivorship policy for the supplier entity type.
sage-intacct is preferred for contact fields (email, phone) because
Intacct's AP module tends to capture richer contact data than X3.
For identity fields (vendor_id, full_name) both sources are authoritative
for their own records; first_non_null handles any nulls.
output_fields defines the canonical supplier schema exposed to BI/analytics.
match_run_id) are always added by the publisher regardless of this list.
When a third supplier source is integrated, add it to source_priority for
each attribute_rule.  No code changes required.
Source IDs in source_priority must match entity extraction config source_id:

attribute_rules._comment: Primary key — always from the originating source, never null (raise_error in field mappings).
Legal name of the supplier. Both sources are authoritative; prefer Intacct as it is more frequently updated.
Email address. Intacct captures AP contact emails more reliably.
Phone number. Prefer most recently updated.
Billing country — ISO country code. Prefer Intacct (billing address is AP-managed).
Transaction currency. Take most recently modified.
Active flag. If either source marks supplier inactive, treat as inactive (most conservative).
Creation timestamp — earliest represents true first-seen date.
Last modification timestamp. Most recently updated source wins.
